one_line: A master Chola bronze artisan of Thanjavur, guiding temple bronzes from wax model to sanctified procession alongside the temple’s patrons.

appearance.age_look: Late 20s, lean but strong, wiry artisan’s build with defined forearms and shoulders.
appearance.face: Oval face, warm medium-brown complexion, slightly aquiline nose, sharp dark eyes with thick brows, a small healed burn mark on the left cheek; default expression is focused and calm with a quiet, confident half-smile.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair gathered into a tight low bun at the back of the head, front kept smooth with a center part; a plain ochre cotton headband tied around the forehead to keep sweat back, knot at the rear under the bun.
appearance.costume: Simple yet dignified artisan attire: a undyed off-white cotton veshti (dhoti) wrapped to mid-calf with a broad deep-maroon border, pleats neatly tucked at the front; bare upper body except for a thin, faded reddish-brown cotton angavastram (shawl) diagonally over the left shoulder and under the right arm; a narrow waist cord in natural fibre tied over the veshti; feet in plain brown leather sandals; minimal jewellery—a small copper stud in the left ear, a thin bronze bangle on the right wrist, and a simple black-thread amulet at the neck; a faint smear of ash (vibhuti) in three horizontal lines on the forehead and right arm, subtly indicating temple devotion.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly soot-darkened bronze pouring ladle with a shallow oval bowl and straight handle, held in his right hand, and a small palm-sized wax model of a dancing deity in his left hand, the wax figure already detailed with tiny ornaments.

ref_desc: Manikkam of Thanjai is a lean late-20s Chola bronze artisan with warm medium-brown skin, oval face, center-parted black hair in a low bun under an ochre headband, wearing an off-white veshti with deep-maroon border, reddish-brown angavastram, minimal copper and bronze ornaments, and carrying a soot-darkened bronze pouring ladle and a small wax dancing-deity model in warm bronze tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character sheet of Manikkam of Thanjai, a late-20s Chola bronze artisan from Thanjavur: lean, wiry build, warm medium-brown skin, oval face, aquiline nose, sharp dark eyes with thick brows, and a small healed burn mark on his left cheek, expression focused and calmly confident. His thick black hair is center-parted and gathered into a tight low bun at the back, with a plain ochre cotton headband tied around his forehead and knotted under the bun to keep sweat back. He wears a historically accurate Imperial Chola artisan outfit: an undyed off-white cotton veshti (dhoti) wrapped to mid-calf with a broad deep-maroon border and neat pleats tucked at the front, bare upper body except for a thin, faded reddish-brown cotton angavastram draped diagonally from his left shoulder across his torso and under his right arm, a simple natural-fibre waist cord over the veshti, and plain brown leather sandals on his feet. Add minimal jewellery only: a small copper stud in his left ear, a thin bronze bangle on his right wrist, and a simple black-thread amulet at his neck, plus faint three-line ash (vibhuti) marks on his forehead and right arm to show his temple connection. In his right hand he holds a long, slightly soot-darkened bronze pouring ladle with a shallow oval bowl and straight handle, and in his left hand he delicately supports a palm-sized wax model of a dancing deity already detailed with tiny ornaments; use an off-white, deep maroon, reddish-brown, warm bronze, and medium brown skin palette, and show him front, side, and back to clearly display hair, costume, and his tools.
